frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: These days every discussion becomes 'men are unsafe' as if all of us are the same. I have spent my whole life in uniform with discipline and respect, and frankly it is unfair when women act like every decent man is a threat. Caution is fine, but this constant suspicion of all men is also not healthy.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering male hurt and insisting that general caution toward men is unfair. It encodes the classic 'not all men' response and frames women's wariness as irrational and insulting to 'decent' men.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 50. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your daughter Neha who is 26 just told you that her male colleague at work has been making advances toward her even though he is married. She is uncomfortable and does not know how to handle it. You are giving her your advice. But your advice ends up focusing on what Neha might be doing that is giving this man the wrong signals rather than on his behavior.
